evidence.claim: Used Hugging Face Transformers, pgvector, Weaviate, OpenSearch, Weights & Biases, scikit-learn, LlamaIndex, Qdrant, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, BM25, and PyTorch
evidence.source_text: Skills: Hugging Face Transformers... pgvector... Weaviate... OpenSearch... Weights & Biases... scikit-learn... LlamaIndex... Qdrant... MongoDB... Elasticsearch... BM25... PyTorch

evidence.claim: Evolved e-commerce search ranking from hand-tuned scoring to learning-to-rank model
evidence.source_text: Owned the ranking layer for an e-commerce search product, evolving it from a hand-tuned scoring function to a learning-to-rank model over 9 months.

evidence.claim: Improved revenue-per-search by 12% via ranking model
Trained and shipped ranking models using XGBoost and LightGBM
evidence.source_text: Trained and shipped multiple ranking models for our product's discovery feed using XGBoost and LightGBM.

evidence.claim: Implemented RAG-based chatbot with document ingestion and answer-generation layers
evidence.source_text: Implemented a RAG-based customer support chatbot... Built the document ingestion pipeline (chunking, embedding via OpenAI embeddings, storing in Pinecone) and the answer-generation layer (initially GPT-4, then a fine-tuned smaller model for cost control).

evidence.claim: Reduced average ticket resolution time by 31%
evidence.source_text: Deployment cut average ticket resolution time by 31% for the supported categories.

evidence.claim: Collaborated with PMs to define optimization targets
evidence.source_text: Worked closely with PMs to define the optimization target (click-through vs. dwell time vs. downstream conversion) — that work was as important as the modeling itself.

evidence.claim: Owns the evaluation framework for RAG-based features
evidence.source_text: Recently, I shipped our first RAG-based feature this year and now own the eval framework for it.
